data.title: Eurozone annual inflation rate down to 1.7% in September
data.description:
• Annual inflation rate in the Euro Are was revised lower to 1.7% in September, compared to initial estimates of 1.8%, and 2.2% in August.
  • CPI decreased 0.1% in September 2024 over the previous month.

data.title: Nokia in charts: Q3 results hit by weak Mobile Networks performance
Tencent Music Entertainment inks strategic partnership with Galaxy
data.description:
• Tencent Music Entertainment (NYSE:TME) inked a strategic partnership with Galaxy Corporation, the agency representing the musician G-Dragon, for his upcoming regional tour.
  • The collaboration marks a significant step forward in TME's (TME) ambitions to expand its global presence.
  • As part of this agreement, TME will act as the proprietary tour partner for G-Dragon's concerts across various markets, including most of Southeast Asia, the Middle East, Hong Kong, Macao, Taiwan, Australia, and New Zealand.
  • TME -1.89% premarket to $11.9.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ABB misses Q3 top and bottom-line estimates
data.description:
• ABB press release (OTCPK:ABBNY): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.51 misses by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $8.15B (+2.4% Y/Y) misses by $210M.
  • Orders $8,193 million, +2%; comparable1 +2%
  • Income from operations $1,309 million; margin 16.1%
  • Operational EBITA1 $1,553 million; margin119.0%
  • Cash flow from operating activities $1,345 million; 0%
  • Outlook: In the fourth quarter of 2024, we anticipate a low to midsingle-digit comparable revenue growth and the historicalpattern to repeat for a negative book-to-bill and asequentially lower Operational EBITA margin
  • In full-year 2024, we expect a positive book-to-bill,comparable revenue growth to be below 5% and theOperational EBITA margin to be slightly above 18%.

data.title: Taiwan Semiconductor in charts: 3nm, 5nm, 7nm, and 16nm chips propel Q3 revenue growth
Germany's DAX touches record high ahead of ECB decision
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.04% as losses in mining stocks offset gains in other sectors.

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.56% to trade around the 19550 level on Thursday, a fresh record high, tracking a general positive mood in Europe.

France (CAC:IND) +0.47% rebounding from losses in the previous session.

Italy's FTSE MIB advanced by 0.9%, surpassing the 34,900 mark on Thursday, trading at its highest level in over four months.

The annual inflation rate in Austria fell to 1.8% in September 2024.

The trade surplus in Switzerland slightly narrowed to CHF 3.9 billion in September.

Norway’s industrial confidence indicator fell to 1.5 in Q3.

The Netherlands’ seasonally adjusted unemployment rate stood at 3.7% in September.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.13% higher, as traders await the monetary policy decision from the ECB and digest fresh corporate results. The central bank is expected to lower interest rates by a quarter point for a second consecutive meeting today, as inflation is now below the 2% target and there are signs the economy is weakening.

Coming up in the session: Euro Area Balance of Trade, inflation and ECB decision due today.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.04%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 2.21%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 3 basis points to 4.10%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: BHP reports higher copper, iron ore production in September quarter
data.description:
Mining giant BHP (NYSE:BHP) Thursday said that copper production was up 4% in the first quarter due to higher grade and recoveries at Escondida, while Western Australia iron ore production was up 3% driven by the completion of debottlenecking work at the port.

Total copper output increased 4% to 476 kt. Copper production guidance for FY25 remains unchanged at between 1,845 and 2,045 kt.

The world’s largest miner produced 64.6 million tons of iron ore over the three months to the end of September. Full-year guidance for its iron ore operations was kept unchanged at 255 to 265.5 million tons.

Regarding steelmaking coal, the Australian miner said it was seeing signs of stabilization in the business with production up 20% in the quarter, excluding the recently divested Blackwater and Daunia mines.

BHP said production was up across all major commodities in its portfolio, adding, China's upcoming stimulus will "likely to focus on relieving local debt, stabilizing the property market and bolstering business confidence.”

"China has announced a series of monetary easing policies in an effort to support economic growth, and has indicated more significant fiscal stimulus is on the horizon," Mike Henry, CEO of BHP (BHP) said in a statement.

BHP shares fell in Wednesday's trading on news that CEO Mike Henry reportedly met with government officials in South Africa last week, prompting speculation that the miner will resurrect its failed attempt to acquire Anglo American (OTCQX:AAUKF) (OTCQX:NGLOY) after the standstill period ends on November 29.

data.title: Meta laying off employees at WhatsApp, Instagram - report
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) has initiated layoffs across various divisions, specifically targeting teams within WhatsApp, Instagram, and Reality Labs, The Verge reported early on Thursday, citing people with knowledge of the matter.

Although the exact number of job cuts has not been disclosed, reports indicate that they are relatively small in scale.

“Today, a few teams at Meta are making changes to ensure resources are aligned with their long-term strategic goals and location strategy,” company spokesperson Dave Arnold told The Verge. “This includes moving some teams to different locations, and moving some employees to different roles. In situations like this when a role is eliminated, we work hard to find other opportunities for impacted employees,” he added.

Notably, these layoffs follow a broader trend of workforce reductions at Meta (META), which has seen ~21,000 jobs cut since late 2022 and are a part of the broader cost-cutting initiatives, with CEO Mark Zuckerberg labeling 2023 as the "year of efficiency."

Meta (META) stock has gained about 80% in one year of trade and gets a Seeking Alpha quant rating of 'Hold' with the highest factor grades marked for profitability.

The stock gets a Wall Street analysts' rating of Strong Buy, with an average price target of $605.62.

Meta (META) is set to release its Q3 earning results on Wednesday, October 30, after market close. Consensus estimate for its Q3 revenue is $40.27B, representing ~18% Y/Y growth.
